items.headline: Global Markets Exhibit Caution Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Anticipated Fed Decision
items.summary: Global markets are displaying a cautious sentiment today, with U.S. futures showing slight declines after a Monday rebound, while Asian markets are mixed. Investors are closely monitoring the ongoing geopolitical conflict in West Asia and the impending Federal Reserve policy decision. The Reserve Bank of Australia, however, raised interest rates, citing inflationary pressures. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Sable Offshore Corp. Leads March Gainers
items.summary: Sable Offshore Corp. is the best-performing stock in March 2026, as of March 17.

items.headline: Amprius Technologies Continues Strong March Performance
items.summary: Amprius Technologies, Inc. ranks among the top gainers for March 2026.

items.headline: Pantoro Gold Ltd. Surges on Gold Demand
items.summary: Australian gold miner Pantoro Gold Ltd. spiked as gold stocks were in high demand on Tuesday.

items.headline: GMDC Up on Rare Earth Elements Collaboration
items.summary: GMDC gained 3% after signing an MoU with NMDC Limited to collaborate on developing an integrated Rare Earth Elements value chain.

items.headline: Redington Down Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Redington fell over 4% after informing that geopolitical tensions in the Gulf region are causing logistical delays and increased freight costs for its subsidiary.

items.headline: Infosys Drops in Early Trading
items.summary: Infosys declined in early Indian trading on Tuesday, March 17, 2026.

items.headline: HCL Technologies Slips
items.summary: HCL Technologies experienced a slip in early Indian trading on Tuesday.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Gathers Amid Inflation and Geopolitical Headwinds
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meets today, March 17-18, with a policy statement expected tomorrow. The Fed is widely anticipated to hold interest rates steady at 3.50%-3.75%, but will assess the economic impact of the Iran conflict, surging oil prices, and new global tariffs. Political pressure for rate cuts is also a factor.

items.headline: Reserve Bank of Australia Raises Interest Rates to 4.1%
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia increased its cash rate by 25 basis points to 4.1% on Tuesday. This decision was driven by persistent local inflation and concerns over the inflationary effects of the Middle East conflict, leading to higher fuel prices.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Continues Towards Value and Defensive Stocks
items.summary: A trend of sector rotation is observable, with capital shifting towards asset-heavy and defensive sectors such as Energy, Utilities, Materials, and Industrials. This comes as Technology and Consumer Discretionary sectors show weakness, partly influenced by AI disruption fears and geopolitical risks.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge as Geopolitical Tensions Persist
items.summary: Crude oil prices continue to climb, with Brent crude reaching nearly $103.17 a barrel and WTI crude at $96.20 on Tuesday. The ongoing conflict in the Middle East and concerns over supply disruptions are primary drivers.

items.headline: Key Companies Report Earnings Today
items.summary: Lufax ($LU) and PSQ Holdings ($PSQH) have announced their Q4 2025 earnings today. Additionally, earnings reports are anticipated from companies including Lululemon, Docusign, and Oklo.

items.headline: U.S. Considers New High Tariffs, Raising Stagflation Concerns
items.summary: The United States is investigating new high-tariff policies, a move that could escalate global economic turmoil. This comes amidst soaring energy prices and growing worries of worldwide stagflation, where rising prices coincide with an economic downturn.
